themes: model competition (GPT-5.5, Grok Build, Codex continuation)

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's token counter tool confirms Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x tokens for text and up to 3x for images vs 4.6 — same per-token price means a real, hidden cost increase that builders need to budget for.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image cost spike is resolution-dependent, not universal — matters for anyone pricing image-heavy Claude workflows.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Field report of Opus 4.7 confidently hallucinating and refusing to back down, forcing a rollback to 4.6 — a real reliability signal for teams deciding whether to upgrade.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick argues OpenAI's decision to publicly release o1 rather than hoard the reasoning breakthrough was the pivotal moment that accelerated the whole field, including DeepSeek.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build and Grok Computer expected to ship together as a unified desktop app — the competitive coding/agent surface is about to get more crowded.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The sharpest one-liner on AI product velocity: if your roadmap exceeds 30 days, you're already behind — 87k views suggests it's landing with builders.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Argues every human system (finance, comms, time mgmt) must be rebuilt from first principles for agents, not just ported — directly relevant to agent-native product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: 30-min workshop from the creator of Claude Code on vibe-coding — highest engagement in the feed and a direct practitioner source worth checking if Claude Code is in your stack.

voices.why: Doing the actual measurement work on Opus 4.7 token costs with a real tool — precise, practitioner-level signal with no hype.
Providing historical context on OpenAI's reasoner release that reframes the competitive landscape; relevant for AI strategy thinking.
Sharp, high-engagement takes on agent-native product strategy and distribution that directly match operator interests.

examples: @aakashgupta spam-posting unrelated trivia (tennis longevity, NASA splashdown, Nolan movie, artillery shells)
crypto/trading pump content (Polymarket bots, AAVE exploit, weather trader)
engagement-bait filler posts (GM CT, LOCK TF IN, WHAT ARE WE APING IN)
